mentions.raw_name: Emma (serving wench at the Quill and Tankard)
Mollander's father (unnamed knight, died at the Blackwater)
Daenerys Targaryen (the Stormborn, the Mad King's daughter)
Aegon (son of Rhaegar, killed as infant)
Ser Moryn Tyrell (Commander of Oldtown City Watch)
Lord Leyton Hightower (Old Man of Oldtown)
Spotted Pate the pig boy (folk-tale character)
Aegon the Conqueror (referenced as "Aegon" who landed at King's Landing)
The Quill and Tankard (inn on island in the Honeywine)
The Guildhalls (west bank of the Honeywine)
The Iron Throne (referenced as object/seat of power)
House Tyrell (Leo's house; Mace Tyrell, Lord of Highgarden; Ser Moryn Tyrell)
House Targaryen (Daenerys, Viserys, Rhaegar, Aegon, Aegon III, the Conqueror)
The Citadel (maesters' order — archmaesters, maesters, acolytes, novices)
The City Watch of Oldtown (commanded by Ser Moryn Tyrell)
Faith of the Seven (multiple septs in Oldtown: Starry Sept, Sailor's Sept, Lord's Sept, Seven Shrines; morning bells; septons mentioned)
R'hllor (the Lord of Light) (red priests at wharfside temple; "the night is dark and full of terrors"; nightfires; Stannis worships him; fiery heart sigil)
"Queer gods" at sailors' temples (Marwyn sacrifices to them)
Ibbenese ("hairy Ibbenese" that Marwyn speaks with)
Dothraki (mentioned in context of dragon reports — "Dothraki dragons")
Rivermen, seamen (patrons of the Quill and Tankard)
Glass candles (obsidian, from Valyria — one green, three black)
The black iron key (opens every door at the Citadel)
Golden dragon coin (three-headed dragon / dead king)
Armen's partial maester chain (pewter, tin, lead, copper links)
Walgrave's strongbox (with contents: silver stags, lock of yellow hair, painted miniature, knight's gauntlet of lobstered steel)
Maester's chains / links (pewter, tin, lead, copper, silver, black iron, Valyrian steel, yellow gold mentioned)
The iron mask (worn by whoever sits in an archmaester's place — Gormon wears Walgrave's)
Pate's savings (nine silver stags, pot of copper stars and pennies)
Old Valyrian scrolls (in the locked vaults)
Leo's bravo's blade and dagger (referenced as weapons he's trained with)
Stannis's banners (fiery heart of R'hllor replacing the crowned stag)
Stories of Spotted Pate the pig boy (folk tales — "a thousand ribald stories")

mentions.raw_name: Red priests' song/prayer ("For the night is dark and full of terrors")

mentions.raw_name: Valyrian scrolls in the Citadel's locked vaults (said to be only surviving copies in the world)

mentions.raw_name: Glass candles (obsidian candles from Valyria, one is burning in Marwyn's chambers — supposedly impossible to light)
Dragonglass / obsidian ("The smallfolk call it dragonglass." "Somehow that seemed important.")
Dragons (three living dragons hatched by Daenerys, reported by sailors)
Warlocks and shadowbinders (Marwyn studied with them in the east)
"Higher mysteries" (what some acolytes study at the Citadel)
"Old powers waken. Shadows stir." (Leo's claim about what the glass candle signifies)
The Doom of Valyria (referenced as temporal marker)
War of the Five Kings (ongoing; Archmaester Benedict disputes the name)
Battle of the Blackwater (Tywin smashed Stannis; Mollander's father died there)
Archmaester (senior rank at the Citadel — Walgrave, Marwyn, Vaellyn, Ebrose, Perestan, Ryam, Benedict)
Acolyte (Armen, Alleras — students with chain links)
Novice (Pate, Mollander, Roone — students without chain links)
High Septon (former seat at the Starry Sept)
Protector of the Citadel (Lord Leyton Hightower)
Commander of the City Watch of Oldtown (Ser Moryn Tyrell)
King (Aegon III, Aegon the Conqueror, Stannis, Balon, Renly — various claimants)
Tiles (a game played at the Checkered Hazard — Leo lost)
The acolyte vigil (Citadel tradition: spending the night in darkness with a glass candle before taking vows)
White ravens (Walgrave's pride; know Pate's name; mutter "Pate, Pate, Pate" — associated with the Citadel and seasonal announcements)
Nightingale (singing in the apple tree; Pate hears it trilling "gold for iron")
